LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS OSCAR HERNANDEZ, JOSE HERNANDEZ, MARIA DOMINGUEZ'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT STRIKES PLAINTIFF'S LATE-FILED OPPOSITION AND ORDERS PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL TO MEET AND CONFER WITH DEFENDANT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, SECTION 430.41. THE COURT CONTINUES THE DEMURRER FOR HEARING TO FEBRUARY 26, 2026. IF THE PARTIES ARE UNABLE TO RESOLVE THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE DEMURRER AFTER MEETING AND CONFERRING, PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A TIMELY OPPOSITION BASED ON THE NEW HEARING DATE. PLAINTIFF SHALL ALSO INCLUDE A DECLARATION CONFIRMING THAT INFORMAL RESOLUTION EFFORTS OCCURRED PURSUANT TO THE CODE. DEFENDANTS MAY FILE A TIMELY REPLY. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. Matter is not contested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS OSCAR HERNANDEZ, JOSE HERNANDEZ, MARIA DOMINGUEZ'S DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiff's first amended complaint is overruled.

A demurrer tests whether the challenged cause of action necessarily fails to state a claim for relief. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019) 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) The court does not assess the plausibility of plaintiff's facts on demurrer.

Here, the accident occurred on or about April 16, 2023. (FAC, para. 21.) Plaintiff alleges the ultimate fact that "the full extent of Plaintiffs' injuries, and the cause thereof, were not discovered, and could not reasonably have been discovered, until on or after September 2, 2023 . . . ." (Id., para. 22.) There is no particularity required for pleading delayed discovery.

Accepting that fact as true, plaintiff has alleged that the accrual of the limitations period was tolled through September 2023, less than two years before the complaint was first filed in August 2025.

Proposed order is submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-26-2026 Continued To Feb-26-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS OSCAR HERNANDEZ, JOSE HERNANDEZ, MARIA DOMINGUEZ'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

The court strikes plaintiff's late-filed opposition and orders plaintiff's counsel to meet and confer with defendant pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, section 430.41. The court continues the demurrer for hearing to February 26, 2026. If the parties are unable to resolve the issues raised by the demurrer after meeting and conferring, plaintiff may file a timely opposition based on the new hearing date. Plaintiff shall also include a declaration confirming that informal resolution efforts occurred pursuant to the Code. Defendants may file a timely reply.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
